emotional_arc.assessment: Unknown — no behavioral data available at session opening

accumulated_interests.interest: Documentation and transparency in co-parenting finances
Respect for boundaries around financial requests
Clear process for what constitutes legitimate shared expenses
Documentation standards that protect against surprise claims

risk_signal_history.signal: Early complaint framing may indicate pattern of grievance
Unilateral complaint without context — Party B has not yet spoken
Has driven both turns of substantive content — may be dominating the conversation space
Framing problem unilaterally without exploring Party B's perspective

relational_posture_progression.posture: Unknown — session has not yet begun

scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: Indeterminate — no interaction has occurred yet

asymmetric_monologue — Party A has framed a grievance, Party B has not responded. Cannot assess relational pattern without hearing from both parties. Risk of session becoming one-sided complaint forum rather than bilateral negotiation.

asymmetric_participation_with_compliance_risk — Party A has established the problem frame across two turns; Party B entered only to defend against that frame. Party B has not independently contributed interests, concerns, or perspective. This creates risk that any solutions will be Party A's solutions that Party B merely agrees to rather than genuinely bilateral outcomes.

notes: Turn 1 represents session opening before any party statements
No behavioral data exists to assess psychological state
Perception will develop as parties begin to speak and interact
Standard session opening assumes provisional stability but mediator must remain alert to signals that emerge immediately
Turn 3 is extremely early — only one substantive party contribution received
Party A's complaint is specific (reimbursement process) but context-free
No information about Party B's perspective, circumstances, or response to complaint
Cannot assess power dynamic, coercion risk, or relational health without hearing from Party B
Premature to infer Party A's underlying interests beyond surface complaint
Party B's single contribution was entirely reactive — defending against 'total control' rather than articulating what they need
The participation pattern is classic asymmetric mediation risk: one party shapes the agenda, the other responds to it
Party B's interests remain fundamentally unknown — we have one defensive statement, not an understanding of what matters to them
Moving to options now would risk creating Party A's solution with Party B's acquiescence rather than genuine bilateral agreement
